topic.name: Energy Tax Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to tax incentives, credits, and regulations aimed at promoting renewable energy and regulating greenhouse gas emissions.

Document 1: repeal tax credits for alternative fuel mixtures
revised income tax rates for corporations based upon energy tax expenditures
Document 2: extend tax credits for producing electricity from renewable resources
denies major integrated oil companies a tax deduction for domestic production
Document 3: establish within the EPA the Federal Emissions Inventory Office
developing a greenhouse gas emission inventory to measure and verify emissions

topic.name: Water and Fisheries Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving and managing water resources and fisheries in the United States.

Document 1: Fishing Economy Improvement Act revises and reauthorizes the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act.
Fishery management plans must include descriptions of subsistence or small scale traditional fishing.
Document 2: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water distribution systems.
Authorizes appropriations for the design, planning, and construction of recycled water system facilities.

topic.name: Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to health care financing, prescription drug management, and health savings accounts.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the taxation of health savings accounts (HSAs)
allows individuals eligible for hospital care or medical services under a medical care program of the Indian Health Service or of a tribal organization to participate in an HSA
Document 2: provides for payments to physicians for writing electronic prescriptions
Document 3: requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to negotiate with pharmaceutical manufacturers the prices that may be charged to prescription drug plan sponsors

topic.name: Legislative Reforms and Protections
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at protecting employee rights, ensuring fair voting practices, and regulating debt collection activities.

Document 1: Requires a labor organization to provide all employees covered by a collective bargaining agreement with written notice explaining dues calculations.
Prohibits a labor organization from collecting dues from non-union employees without their consent.
Document 2: Amends the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act to require states to transmit absentee ballots by a specific deadline.
Allows any person aggrieved by a violation of the Act to bring a civil action for necessary declaratory or injunctive relief.
Document 3: Amends the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act to exclude certain legal professionals from the definition of 'debt collector.'
Communicating in court or during depositions related to debt collection is not considered debt collection under the Act.

topic.name: Housing Assistance Programs
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts aimed at providing and regulating housing assistance for individuals with disabilities and low-income families.

Document 1: Amends the Cranston-Gonzalez National Affordable Housing Act to revise requirements for supportive housing for persons with disabilities.
Authorizes appropriations for rental assistance under section 8 for persons with disabilities.
Document 2: Amends the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 to make households receiving state assistance eligible for supplemental nutrition assistance program (SNAP).
Document 3: Instructs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to provide 150,000 incremental vouchers for tenant-based rental housing assistance.

topic.name: Tenth Amendment and Federal Regulation
topic.description: Discussion on state authority to challenge federal regulations under the Tenth Amendment.

Document 1: Authorizes a designated state official to submit to the head of a federal agency proposing a rule, during the period when the proposed rule is open for public comment, a legal brief challenging the constitutionality of the rule under the Tenth Amendment.

Permits such state official, in addition to any other venue or jurisdiction provided by law, to bring such action in the U.S. district court for the district in which the official's place of business is located.
Document 3: Authorizes a designated state official to file with the head of a federal agency proposing a rule, during the period when the proposed rule is required to be open for public comment, a legal brief challenging the constitutionality of the rule under the Tenth Amendment.
Directs the relevant U.S. Court of Appeals, at the request of a designated state official, to grant expedited review of a decision by a district court in such a case.

topic.name: Consumer Privacy in Telecommunications
topic.description: Legislation focused on protecting consumer information and privacy rights in the telecommunications sector.

Document 1: Makes it unlawful to acquire or use customer proprietary network information without written consent
Prohibits providers from publishing the information without such authorization.
Document 2: Prohibits a person from searching telephony metadata acquired pursuant to an order
Defines 'telephony metadata' to mean communications routing information.
Document 3: Requires each call center employee to disclose the physical location of such employee at the beginning of such call
Subjects noncomplying corporations or subsidiaries to such civil penalties as the FTC may prescribe.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Consumer Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating financial institutions and protecting consumers in the financial sector.

Document 1: Amends the Judicial Code to prescribe the venue for cases involving non-bank financial institutions.
Establishes the Market Stability and Capital Adequacy Board to monitor the interactions of various sectors of the financial system.
Document 2: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to limit the total credit which may be extended by a creditor to a college student.
Prohibits increasing the credit limit on an account without written approval from a responsible party.
Document 3: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act with respect to minimum leverage and capital requirements.
Declares that federal banking agencies shall not be required to subject certain entities to minimum requirements if regulated by state insurance regulators.

topic.name: Labor and Economic Development Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing labor-management relations, promoting economic growth, and fostering innovation within specific industries.

Document 1: Establishes the Federal Labor-Management Partnership Council to advise the President on matters involving labor-management relations in the executive branch.
involve employees and their representatives as full partners with management representatives to improve the civil service to better serve the public
Document 2: Directs the President to establish an interagency mechanism to improve coordination of U.S. development programs with private sector investment activities.
ensure that independent analyses of constraints to growth are conducted as a component of all appropriate country, sector, and global development strategies.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to establish a grant program to fund textile research and innovation in the U.S. textile and fiber products industry.
build and sustain innovation and competitiveness in the U.S. textile industry.

topic.name: Post Office Designations
topic.description: The documents discuss the formal designation of various United States Postal Service facilities in honor of individuals.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 5720 South 142nd Street in Omaha, Nebraska, as the 'Petty Officer 1st Class Caleb A. Nelson Post Office Building.'
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 830 Kuhn Drive in Chula Vista, California, as the 'Jonathan 'J.D.' De Guzman Post Office Building.'
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 200 North Texas Avenue in Odessa, Texas, as the 'Corporal Alfred Mac Wilson Post Office.'

topic.name: Military Family and Veteran Benefits
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and policies aimed at providing support and benefits to military families and veterans, including leave entitlements, healthcare eligibility, and educational assistance.

Document 1: entitles an employed family member of a member of the Armed Forces to two workweeks of leave per year
provides employment and benefits protection for employees upon their return from such leave
Document 2: eliminates the requirement that a member or former member of the Armed Forces entitled to retired pay for non-regular service be at least 60 years of age for medical care
Document 3: includes certain full-time active duty service as qualifying service for veterans educational assistance purposes

topic.name: Mental Health Services Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving mental health services and support for individuals and caregivers in the United States.

Document 1: persons with a degree that is equivalent to a master's degree in mental health counseling shall be eligible for appointment
Department of Veterans Affairs licensed professional mental health counselor position
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public and nonprofit private health care providers
expand training and support services for families and caregivers of patients with Alzheimer's disease
Document 3: requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to enter into an agreement with the Institute of Medicine
gaps in mental health treatment services for adults and children with severe and persistent mental illness

topic.name: Educational Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing support for students and educational institutions through funding, resources, and protections related to education.

Document 3: Academic Partnerships Lead Us to Success Act
allows each state to submit to the Secretary of Education a declaration of intent, applicable for up to five years, permitting it to receive federal funds on a consolidated basis.

topic.name: Tribal Sovereignty and Federal Relations
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legal recognition and rights of Native American tribes in relation to federal laws and benefits, including land trust issues and tribal governance.

Document 1: Extends all federal laws and regulations to the Duwamish Tribe.
Makes the Tribe and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government to federally recognized tribes.
Document 2: Authorizes Utah to relinquish for the benefit of the Ute Indian Tribe certain of its school or subsurface mineral lands.
in exchange for certain federal subsurface mineral lands.
Document 3: Prohibits consideration of an entity's authorization under federal, state, or tribal law to produce, purchase, or possess marijuana when allocating, distributing, or determining eligibility for federal benefits.

topic.name: Support for Small Businesses
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing opportunities and resources for small businesses, particularly those owned by veterans and those affected by disasters.

Document 1: Improving Contracting Opportunities for Veteran-Owned Small Businesses Act of 2012
directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to include the acquisition of goods and services through federal supply schedules for veteran-owned businesses.
Document 2: Disaster Surety Bond Enhancement Act of 2007
permits the SBA to guarantee surety bonds for federal procurement related to major disasters.
Document 3: SBA Loan Paperwork Reduction Act of 2012
directs the SBA to carry out a Small Loan Advantage Program to benefit underserved markets.

topic.name: Immigration Reform Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative amendments and acts related to immigration policy and citizenship processes in the United States.

Document 1: Citizenship Promotion Act of 2007 - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act
establish a national citizenship promotion program to assist lawful permanent resident aliens become citizens.
Document 2: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to provide that a returning H-2B alien
shall be considered a returning worker.
Document 3: Ensuring Congressional Oversight of Immigration Act
terminates the authority exercised by the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) through U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS)

topic.name: Legislative Measures on Security and Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at addressing security issues and individual rights related to firearms, military sexual trauma, and terrorism prevention.

Document 1: establish a national standard for the carrying of concealed firearms
carry a concealed firearm in another state
Document 2: require the Director of the Sexual Assault Prevention and Response Office to be appointed
entitles any member, or dependent of a member, who is the victim of a sexual assault to legal assistance
Document 3: impose a fine and/or prison term for providing material support to international terrorism
increases prison terms for providing material support to terrorists

topic.name: Government Funding Allocations
topic.description: This topic encompasses the allocation and use of government funds derived from fines, penalties, and loans for specific public welfare initiatives.

Document 1: Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) to retain amounts collected for safety-related fines
distribute them to counties for maintaining radiological emergency preparedness plans
Document 2: Appropriates specified funds from any amounts received by the Treasury as a civil monetary penalty
assist in providing affordable housing or to mitigate indirect costs relating to foreclosures on residential mortgages
Document 3: permits the Department of Health and Human Services to make loans and loan guarantees for construction or renovation of psychiatric or substance abuse treatment facilities
establishes the Mental Health and Substance Use Treatment Trust Fund

topic.name: Government Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at enhancing government accountability, transparency, and oversight in various federal activities and programs.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to conduct a review of, and report to the congressional defense committees on, the backlog of pending cases in DOD's Integrated Disability Evaluation System.
Document 2: Requires federal agencies to report to Congress on any activity to use or develop data mining to identify terrorist or criminal activity.
Requires annual updates, including regarding any new uses or development of data mining activity.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to conduct an in-depth study on the implementation of a transaction fee in the United States to replace all existing federal taxes.
Requires the Secretary to report to Congress on the results of such study within one year after the enactment of this Act.

topic.name: U.S. National Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the policies and actions taken by the U.S. government to address national security threats and military operations.

Document 1: prohibits contracting with entities supporting enemies of the U.S.
requires identification of entities providing funds to enemies
Document 2: repeals military force authorization against Iraq
clarifies presidential authority for U.S. security in Iraq
Document 3: authorizes funding for additional FBI agents
supports national security through enhanced law enforcement

topic.name: Tariff Suspension Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the temporary suspension of duties on specific goods in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on 50 Percent Amide Neutralized Phosphated Polyester Polymer
suspend temporarily the duty on 50 Percent Amide Neutralized Phosphated Polyester Polymer
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain knit-to-shape sweatshirts for women or girls
suspend temporarily the duty on certain knit-to-shape sweatshirts for women or girls
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on the mixtures or coprecipitates of yttrium phosphate or cerium phosphate
suspend temporarily the duty on the mixtures or coprecipitates of yttrium phosphate or cerium phosphate

topic.name: Public Health and Safety Regulations
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislative measures aimed at enhancing public health and safety through regulatory frameworks for transportation, laboratory tests, and drug disposal.

Document 1: authorize the Secretary of Transportation to grant a waiver or exemption from certain airspace restrictions
poses no security risk
Document 2: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a single publicly accessible test registry
information regarding the analytical validity of each test
Document 3: authorize an ultimate user or care taker to dispose of a controlled substance
protect the public safety

topic.name: Environmental Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting natural resources and regulating activities that impact marine and freshwater environments.

Document 3: Amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 to extend a state's shield from federal liability for costs or damages incurred as a result of actions taken or omitted while implementing a state abandoned mine reclamation plan.

topic.name: Student Loan Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation and management of student loans and financial assistance in higher education.

Document 1: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
establish rules that lenders must follow regarding the overpayment and underpayment of student loan payments
Document 3: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require a lender, before issuing a private education loan
to obtain the IHE's certification of the student's enrollment status

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code related to tax deductions and credits for individuals and specific projects.

Document 1: increase the limit on the deductibility of losses from the sale or exchange of capital assets
adjusted for inflation after 2009
Document 2: allow holders of clean renewable water supply bonds a tax credit of 25%
issued by a governmental body or an entity qualified to issue tax-exempt bonds
Document 3: make permanent the tax deduction for income attributable to domestic production activities
in Puerto Rico
